## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Implementation. Single pace, layered scope: Regime → Module → Tabtargets
  → Spec/Integration → Verification.
  
  ## Docket
  
  Land the design from heat paddock and BUS0 spec (post-AAp split:
  fenestrate + garrison sibling verbs sharing one implementation module).
  
  ### L1. Regime & Constants
  
  - Implement four-field BURP, drop `BURN_WORKLOADS`, add
    `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` per paddock.
  - `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` enrolls as xname (1–32), format-checked by buv.
    No collision list, no allowed-set enumeration — it is just the SSH
    login name used to reach a node as the workload operator.
  - `bub` / `bube` / `bubep_*` tree minted; BUBC tinder constants placed
    (`BUBC_platforms_{windows,linux,mac}` holding `bubep_*`); `BURN_PLATFORM`
    re-enrolled with `bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows`.
  
  ### L2. Module & Helper
  
  - `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh` BCG-compliant module per paddock.
    Sub-letter `b` = bash; legend added to BUS0 mapping section per Quoin
    Sub-Letter Discipline.
  - `bujb_resolve_investiture` is sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint,
    exposes `BUJB_RESOLVED_*` globals (set listed in paddock), single-call-
    per-process, readonly after resolution.
  - Helper enforces at resolve-time: `BURP_VICEROYALTY` registered in BURN;
    key files exist with mode `0600`; privkey loads via `ssh-keygen -y` dry-
    load (proves parseable + unencrypted; replaces vague "key-pair self-
    consistency" wording — there is no second artifact, only the privkey);
    WSL default distribution = `rbtww-main`.
  
  ### L3. Tabtargets & Ceremonies
  
  - 14 tabtargets per paddock catalog: 11 net-new + 3 modify-in-place
    (`buw-rn[lrv]` already exist, reshape for new BURN four-field shape;
    `buw-rp[lrv]` are net-new alongside `buw-jpF`, `buw-jpG[bcw]`,
    `buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`, `buw-hj0`).
  - Operational tabtargets call `bujb_resolve_investiture` first, then
    assert per-verb platform invariant inline (2-line check; helper stays
    generic): fenestrate = `bubep_windows`; garrison-b ∈ {`bubep_linux`,
    `bubep_mac`}; garrison-c/w = `bubep_windows`.
  - Fenestrate ceremony per BUSJPF two-phase contract; bash orchestrator
    treats post-restart disconnect as expected phase boundary.
  - Garrison ceremony per BUSJG[BCW] (workload-only, 6 steps); first admin-
    SSH failure surfaces a clear pointer to fenestrate handbook (Windows)
    or operator-managed `ssh-copy-id` (Linux/Mac).
  
  ### L4. Spec & Integration
  
  - BUS0 / BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW specify the three concrete `command=`
    directive strings (b/c/w) as locked spec content, sourced by the
    module rather than invented there. `command=` is a documented OpenSSH
    `authorized_keys(5)` directive; standard usage.
  - BUS0 drift sweep folded in: `:burp_node:` linked term at line 127 →
    `:burp_viceroyalty:` (and any sibling stale `BURP_NODE` / `BURP_USER` /
    `BURP_SSH_PUBKEY` / `BURP_KEY_FILE` / `BURP_COMMAND` references AAp's
    sweep missed). Verify with `grep -n` before completion.
  - Superseded colophons retired (audit `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`, old
    `buw-HW*`); AAm prunes only those directly replaced by the new 14-
    target catalog. Broad sweep deferred to AAn.
  - RBK Windows orchestrator (`rbhw_windows.sh`, `rbw-HWd[cdw]`,
    `rbw-h0`, `rbw-hw`) rewired to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.
  
  ### L5. Verification
  
  - `bash -n` green on all edited shell files.
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
  - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` unchanged from baseline.
  
  ### Out of scope
  
  PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred); adopt verb (deferred); user
  regime (deferred); Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb (deferred — fenestrate
  is Windows-OpenSSH only); shell-letter availability discovery on BURN
  side; end-to-end operator test on a real Windows node (lives in
  AAD/E/F); broad cleanup sweep (lives in AAn); operator data migration
  (no migration — pre-release, nothing working to preserve).

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Implementation. Single pace, layered scope: Regime → Module → Tabtargets
  → Spec/Integration → Verification.
  
  ## Docket
  
  Land the design from heat paddock and BUS0 spec (post-AAp split:
  fenestrate + garrison sibling verbs sharing one implementation module).
  
  ### L1. Regime & Constants
  
  - Implement four-field BURP, drop `BURN_WORKLOADS`, add
    `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` per paddock.
  - `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` enrolls as xname (1–32), format-checked by buv.
    No collision list, no allowed-set enumeration — it is just the SSH
    login name used to reach a node as the workload operator.
  - `bub` / `bube` / `bubep_*` tree minted; BUBC tinder constants placed
    (`BUBC_platforms_{windows,linux,mac}` holding `bubep_*`); `BURN_PLATFORM`
    re-enrolled with `bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows`.
  
  ### L2. Module & Helper
  
  - `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh` BCG-compliant module per paddock.
    Sub-letter `b` = bash; legend added to BUS0 mapping section per Quoin
    Sub-Letter Discipline.
  - `bujb_resolve_investiture` is sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint,
    exposes `BUJB_RESOLVED_*` globals (set listed in paddock), single-call-
    per-process, readonly after resolution.
  - Helper enforces at resolve-time: `BURP_VICEROYALTY` registered in BURN;
    key files exist with mode `0600`; privkey loads via `ssh-keygen -y` dry-
    load (proves parseable + unencrypted; replaces vague "key-pair self-
    consistency" wording — there is no second artifact, only the privkey);
    WSL default distribution = `rbtww-main`.
  
  ### L3. Tabtargets & Ceremonies
  
  - 14 tabtargets per paddock catalog: 11 net-new + 3 modify-in-place
    (`buw-rn[lrv]` already exist, reshape for new BURN four-field shape;
    `buw-rp[lrv]` are net-new alongside `buw-jpF`, `buw-jpG[bcw]`,
    `buw-jwk`, `buw-jwc`, `buw-jws`, `buw-hj0`).
  - Operational tabtargets call `bujb_resolve_investiture` first, then
    assert per-verb platform invariant inline (2-line check; helper stays
    generic): fenestrate = `bubep_windows`; garrison-b ∈ {`bubep_linux`,
    `bubep_mac`}; garrison-c/w = `bubep_windows`.
  - Fenestrate ceremony per BUSJPF two-phase contract; bash orchestrator
    treats post-restart disconnect as expected phase boundary.
  - Garrison ceremony per BUSJG[BCW] (workload-only, 6 steps); first admin-
    SSH failure surfaces a clear pointer to fenestrate handbook (Windows)
    or operator-managed `ssh-copy-id` (Linux/Mac).
  
  ### L4. Spec & Integration
  
  - BUS0 / BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW specify the three concrete `command=`
    directive strings (b/c/w) as locked spec content, sourced by the
    module rather than invented there. `command=` is a documented OpenSSH
    `authorized_keys(5)` directive; standard usage.
  - BUS0 drift sweep folded in: `:burp_node:` linked term at line 127 →
    `:burp_viceroyalty:` (and any sibling stale `BURP_NODE` / `BURP_USER` /
    `BURP_SSH_PUBKEY` / `BURP_KEY_FILE` / `BURP_COMMAND` references AAp's
    sweep missed). Verify with `grep -n` before completion.
  - Superseded colophons retired (audit `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`, old
    `buw-HW*`); AAm prunes only those directly replaced by the new 14-
    target catalog. Broad sweep deferred to AAn.
  - RBK Windows orchestrator (`rbhw_windows.sh`, `rbw-HWd[cdw]`,
    `rbw-h0`, `rbw-hw`) rewired to fenestrate + garrison + handbook.
  
  ### L5. Verification
  
  - `bash -n` green on all edited shell files.
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green.
  - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` unchanged from baseline.
  
  ### Out of scope
  
  PowerShell garrison + dispatch (deferred); adopt verb (deferred); user
  regime (deferred); Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb (deferred — fenestrate
  is Windows-OpenSSH only); shell-letter availability discovery on BURN
  side; end-to-end operator test on a real Windows node (lives in
  AAD/E/F); broad cleanup sweep (lives in AAn); operator data migration
  (no migration — pre-release, nothing working to preserve).

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	
8	## Current Concept
9	
10	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
11	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
12	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing both verbs). Neither
13	the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in
14	BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
15	
16	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
17	
18	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
19	  rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
20	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Four fields:
21	  `BURP_VICEROYALTY` (BURN reference), `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`,
22	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`, `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from
23	  privkeys via `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline
24	  strings. Identifier: investiture.
25	
26	**BURC addition:**
27	
28	- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
29	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
30	
31	**Two verbs (Fenestrate, Garrison):**
32	
33	- **Fenestrate** — Windows-OpenSSH-only `sshd_config` harden plus admin SSH
34	  key-trust establishment. Uniform across shell-letters (no variant).
35	  Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` killing its own
36	  session. Tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config application).
37	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per-shell-letter
38	  (b/c/w). Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
39	  authorized_keys. First action is admin SSH via key auth; failure surfaces
40	  immediately.
41	- For Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb — admin trust is operator-manual
42	  (e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); garrison runs against an existing key-trusted admin
43	  foothold. For Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening on Windows nodes (in addition to
44	  Windows OpenSSH), hardening is operator-manual.
45	
46	**Garrison-destructive model:**
47	
48	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
49	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
50	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
51	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
52	- Admin-side sshd configuration is fenestrate's scope on Windows;
53	  operator-manual elsewhere.
54	
55	**Cross-cutting premises:**
56	
57	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
58	  it by reference, never duplicates.
59	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
60	  material; operator owns all key administration.
61	
62	**Tabtarget colophons (14 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
63	
64	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
65	- `buw-jpF` — Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform) — 1
66	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
67	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
68	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
69	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
70	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
71	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1
72	
73	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
74	
75	BCG-compliant module housing both fenestrate and garrison implementations,
76	hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings (b/c/w)
77	used by garrison on the workload account, the workload privkey destination
78	path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name
79	(`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive
80	set used by fenestrate. SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries
81	no shell selection.
82	
83	**Fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform):**
84	
85	Two-phase remote-run because `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own
86	ssh session.
87	
88	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
89	
90	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
91	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
92	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
93	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Fenestrate
94	   process never sees the password.
95	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
96	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
97	   (no `command=`; fenestrate is uniform). Performed before any sshd_config
98	   change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
99	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
100	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
101	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
102	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
103	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
104	   values. Keeps PowerShell out of parsing (its error semantics are
105	   unreliable).
106	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
107	   malformed config aborts fenestrate before it could brick running sshd).
108	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
109	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
110	   expected phase boundary).
111	
112	Phase 2 (key auth only):
113	
114	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
115	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
116	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
117	   a session.
118	9. Verify post-state: the admin session authenticates by key alone and the
119	   running sshd serves the hardened configuration.
120	
121	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
122	
123	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
124	   only (no password fallback). On Windows, presupposes fenestrate has run
125	   for this investiture. On Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-managed
126	   (e.g., `ssh-copy-id` placed the admin pubkey).
127	2. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
128	   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
129	   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
130	3. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
131	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
132	4. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
133	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
134	5. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
135	   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
136	   outbound to GitHub).
137	6. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
138	
139	## Heat Sequence
140	
141	**Spec foundation:**
142	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
143	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
144	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb
145	
146	**Implementation:**
147	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
148	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 14 tabtargets, fenestrate + garrison
149	  ceremonies, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries
150	
151	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
152	- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
153	
154	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
155	- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
156	- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
157	- AAF — Docker dual-daemon
158	
159	## Deferred (named for future return)
160	
161	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
162	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
163	  only.
164	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
165	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
166	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
167	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
168	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
169	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
170	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
171	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
172	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
173	- **Cygwin / WSL sshd hardening verb** — fenestrate covers Windows OpenSSH
174	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
175	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
176	  fenestrate sibling.
177	
178	## Standing Notes
179	
180	- **Fenestrate handles first-time admin trust establishment on Windows.**
181	  On a fresh node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls
182	  through to a /dev/tty password prompt; operator types the Windows admin
183	  password once. Fenestrate then installs the admin pubkey, sets the icacls
184	  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
185	  and reconnects to verify by key. Subsequent runs use key auth
186	  automatically. The operator's manual Windows scope reduces to: install
187	  OpenSSH server, enable the service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes`
188	  temporarily, allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known
189	  password.
190	- **Linux/Mac admin trust is operator-managed.** No fenestrate verb;
191	  operator places admin pubkey via `ssh-copy-id` (or equivalent), and
192	  garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone.
193	- **Two-phase fenestrate is structural, not optional.** `Restart-Service
194	  sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any post-restart verification must
195	  happen over a fresh session. Bash orchestrator treats the post-restart
196	  disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
197	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
198	  prompts interactively; fenestrate and garrison would hang. Operator
199	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
200	  privkey-at-rest protection.
201	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
202	  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
203	  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
204	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
205	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
206	  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
207	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
208	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
209	  path construction.
210	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
211	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
212	- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`.
213	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
214	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
215	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
216	  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing both verbs). Neither
the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing in
BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Four fields:
  `BURP_VICEROYALTY` (BURN reference), `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`,
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`, `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from
  privkeys via `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline
  strings. Identifier: investiture.

**BURC addition:**

- `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.

**Two verbs (Fenestrate, Garrison):**

- **Fenestrate** — Windows-OpenSSH-only `sshd_config` harden plus admin SSH
  key-trust establishment. Uniform across shell-letters (no variant).
  Two-phase ceremony forced by `Restart-Service sshd` killing its own
  session. Tabtarget `buw-jpF` (capital F = persistent config application).
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per-shell-letter
  (b/c/w). Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys. First action is admin SSH via key auth; failure surfaces
  immediately.
- For Linux/Mac there is no fenestrate verb — admin trust is operator-manual
  (e.g., `ssh-copy-id`); garrison runs against an existing key-trusted admin
  foothold. For Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening on Windows nodes (in addition to
  Windows OpenSSH), hardening is operator-manual.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side sshd configuration is fenestrate's scope on Windows;
  operator-manual elsewhere.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (14 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpF` — Fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform) — 1
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top — 1

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing both fenestrate and garrison implementations,
hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings (b/c/w)
used by garrison on the workload account, the workload privkey destination
path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL distribution name
(`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive
set used by fenestrate. SSH transport mechanics live in code; BURP carries
no shell selection.

**Fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only; uniform):**

Two-phase remote-run because `Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own
ssh session.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Fenestrate
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; fenestrate is uniform). Performed before any sshd_config
   change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values. Keeps PowerShell out of parsing (its error semantics are
   unreliable).
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts fenestrate before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-state: the admin session authenticates by key alone and the
   running sshd serves the hardened configuration.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback). On Windows, presupposes fenestrate has run
   for this investiture. On Linux/Mac, admin trust is operator-managed
   (e.g., `ssh-copy-id` placed the admin pubkey).
2. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or platform
   equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files outside home
   are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
3. Create fresh workload user named `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
4. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
5. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded in
   `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can authenticate
   outbound to GitHub).
6. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, 14 tabtargets, fenestrate + garrison
  ceremonies, cleanup superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
- AAF — Docker dual-daemon

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL sshd hardening verb** — fenestrate covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  fenestrate sibling.

## Standing Notes

- **Fenestrate handles first-time admin trust establishment on Windows.**
  On a fresh node, ssh's `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` falls
  through to a /dev/tty password prompt; operator types the Windows admin
  password once. Fenestrate then installs the admin pubkey, sets the icacls
  ACL, hardens sshd_config (`PasswordAuthentication no`), restarts sshd,
  and reconnects to verify by key. Subsequent runs use key auth
  automatically. The operator's manual Windows scope reduces to: install
  OpenSSH server, enable the service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes`
  temporarily, allow the firewall port, ensure the admin user has a known
  password.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust is operator-managed.** No fenestrate verb;
  operator places admin pubkey via `ssh-copy-id` (or equivalent), and
  garrison's first admin SSH succeeds by key alone.
- **Two-phase fenestrate is structural, not optional.** `Restart-Service
  sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any post-restart verification must
  happen over a fresh session. Bash orchestrator treats the post-restart
  disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; fenestrate and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.** Operator
  pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal SSH key)
  before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. If the current default WSL distribution differs,
  garrison-WSL fails fast with a clear error.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion and
  path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite, GitHub,
  rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_fenestrate}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_fenestrate} ceremony — Windows OpenSSH only. Establishes
5	admin SSH key trust and hardens `sshd_config` to key-only
6	authentication on the node, leaving it ready for {busn_garrison} to
7	provision the workload account. Two-phase remote-run because
8	`Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session: phase 1 places
9	admin trust, writes the hardened `sshd_config`, validates it, and
10	restarts sshd; phase 2 reconnects via key auth alone to verify the
11	post-restart state.
12	
13	//axhopt_typed_parameter
14	{burp_investiture}
15	
16	//axhoq_precondition
17	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
18	referenced {burn_regime} profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node.
19	
20	//axhoq_precondition
21	The Windows handbook bootstrap is complete: OpenSSH Server installed,
22	sshd started and set to Automatic, firewall opens the configured SSH
23	port, and `sshd_config` carries `PasswordAuthentication yes` so that
24	fenestrate's phase-1 admin SSH session can fall through to a password
25	prompt on a fresh node.
26	
27	//axhoq_precondition
28	{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group with a
29	known password (typed once on phase-1 first run when ssh prompts on
30	/dev/tty; fenestrate itself never sees the password).
31	
32	//axhoq_precondition
33	{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
34	station.
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
38	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
39	{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates by key. First run: ssh
40	prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Phase 1 — place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
44	(derived from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in
45	Windows OpenSSH `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare key entry
46	(no `command=` directive — fenestrate is uniform across shell-letters).
47	Re-runs converge as a no-op when the line is already present. Performed
48	before any sshd_config change so that key auth is available for
49	phase 2's reconnect.
50	
51	//axhos_step
52	. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys*: apply
53	`icacls` so only SYSTEM and the Administrators group hold ACL
54	entries; Windows OpenSSH refuses to read the file otherwise.
55	Idempotent.
56	
57	//axhos_step
58	. *Phase 1 — write hardened directives*: set `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
59	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` in
60	`sshd_config`. Idempotent — already-hardened configs converge to the
61	same byte sequence on re-run.
62	
63	//axhos_step
64	. *Phase 1 — verify written state*: PowerShell `Get-Content` returns
65	the raw `sshd_config` bytes; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts
66	each effective directive value and asserts it matches the intended
67	hardened value. Keeps PowerShell out of the parsing business (its
68	error semantics are unreliable).
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*: `sshd -t` runs as the penultimate
72	atomic op of phase 1. A malformed config aborts fenestrate before it
73	could brick the running sshd.
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*: `Restart-Service sshd` is the final atomic
77	op of phase 1. The bash orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately
78	after this exec as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
79	
80	//axhos_step
81	. *Phase 2 — reconnect via key auth*: re-establish admin SSH as
82	{burp_privileged_user} with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey`
83	(no password fallback). Failure here surfaces a brick — the operator's
84	manual password no longer opens a session.
85	
86	//axhos_step
87	. *Phase 2 — verify post-state*: confirm the post-restart admin
88	session authenticates by key and the running sshd serves the hardened
89	configuration.
90	
91	//axhog_guarantee
92	On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
93	operator's admin pubkey installed in `administrators_authorized_keys`
94	under correct ACLs, `sshd_config` carries the three hardening
95	directives, and the post-restart admin session has been verified to
96	authenticate by key without password fallback.
97	
98	//axhoc_completion
99	Exit 0 when phase 2 verification succeeds.
100	Non-zero on phase-1 admin SSH failure, pubkey-write failure, ACL
101	lockdown failure, sshd_config write failure, parsed-state mismatch,
102	`sshd -t` failure, or phase-2 key-auth failure.
103

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_fenestrate}

NOTE: {busn_fenestrate} ceremony — Windows OpenSSH only. Establishes
admin SSH key trust and hardens `sshd_config` to key-only
authentication on the node, leaving it ready for {busn_garrison} to
provision the workload account. Two-phase remote-run because
`Restart-Service sshd` terminates its own ssh session: phase 1 places
admin trust, writes the hardened `sshd_config`, validates it, and
restarts sshd; phase 2 reconnects via key auth alone to verify the
post-restart state.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
The Windows handbook bootstrap is complete: OpenSSH Server installed,
sshd started and set to Automatic, firewall opens the configured SSH
port, and `sshd_config` carries `PasswordAuthentication yes` so that
fenestrate's phase-1 admin SSH session can fall through to a password
prompt on a fresh node.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group with a
known password (typed once on phase-1 first run when ssh prompts on
/dev/tty; fenestrate itself never sees the password).

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates by key. First run: ssh
prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — place admin trust*: idempotently install the admin pubkey
(derived from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in
Windows OpenSSH `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare key entry
(no `command=` directive — fenestrate is uniform across shell-letters).
Re-runs converge as a no-op when the line is already present. Performed
before any sshd_config change so that key auth is available for
phase 2's reconnect.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys*: apply
`icacls` so only SYSTEM and the Administrators group hold ACL
entries; Windows OpenSSH refuses to read the file otherwise.
Idempotent.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — write hardened directives*: set `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` in
`sshd_config`. Idempotent — already-hardened configs converge to the
same byte sequence on re-run.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — verify written state*: PowerShell `Get-Content` returns
the raw `sshd_config` bytes; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts
each effective directive value and asserts it matches the intended
hardened value. Keeps PowerShell out of the parsing business (its
error semantics are unreliable).

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*: `sshd -t` runs as the penultimate
atomic op of phase 1. A malformed config aborts fenestrate before it
could brick the running sshd.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*: `Restart-Service sshd` is the final atomic
op of phase 1. The bash orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately
after this exec as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — reconnect via key auth*: re-establish admin SSH as
{burp_privileged_user} with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey`
(no password fallback). Failure here surfaces a brick — the operator's
manual password no longer opens a session.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — verify post-state*: confirm the post-restart admin
session authenticates by key and the running sshd serves the hardened
configuration.

//axhog_guarantee
On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
operator's admin pubkey installed in `administrators_authorized_keys`
under correct ACLs, `sshd_config` carries the three hardening
directives, and the post-restart admin session has been verified to
authenticate by key without password fallback.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when phase 2 verification succeeds.
Non-zero on phase-1 admin SSH failure, pubkey-write failure, ACL
lockdown failure, sshd_config write failure, parsed-state mismatch,
`sshd -t` failure, or phase-2 key-auth failure.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
20	#
21	# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22	# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23	# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24	# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25	# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26	# hardening directive set.
27	#
28	# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation. A future
29	# PowerShell sibling would mint as bujp_jurisdiction.ps1.
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	# Multiple inclusion detection
34	test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
35	ZBUJB_SOURCED=1
36	
37	######################################################################
38	# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)
39	
40	zbujb_kindle() {
41	  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"
42	
43	  # Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
44	  # Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
45	  # behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
46	  # requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
47	  BUJB_command_b='command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
48	  BUJB_command_c='command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
49	  BUJB_command_w='command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
50	
51	  # Shell-letter -> workload privkey destination path on the remote
52	  # (relative to the workload account home directory).
53	  BUJB_workload_keypath_b='.ssh/id_ed25519'
54	  BUJB_workload_keypath_c='.ssh/id_ed25519'
55	  BUJB_workload_keypath_w='.ssh/id_ed25519'
56	
57	  # Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
58	  BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
59	
60	  # Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
61	  # fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
62	  # bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
63	  BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
64	PasswordAuthentication no
65	PermitEmptyPasswords no'
66	
67	  readonly BUJB_command_b BUJB_command_c BUJB_command_w
68	  readonly BUJB_workload_keypath_b BUJB_workload_keypath_c BUJB_workload_keypath_w
69	  readonly BUJB_wsl_distribution
70	  readonly BUJB_sshd_hardening
71	
72	  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
73	}
74	
75	zbujb_sentinel() {
76	  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
77	}
78	
79	# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
80	# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
81	# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label.
82	zbujb_check_key_file() {
83	  local z_path="${1:-}"
84	  local z_varname="${2:-}"
85	
86	  test -n "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
87	  test -f "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"
88	
89	  local z_mode=""
90	  if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
91	    : # macOS / BSD stat
92	  elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
93	    : # GNU/Linux stat
94	  else
95	    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path}"
96	  fi
97	  test "${z_mode}" = "600" || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"
98	
99	  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
100	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path}"
101	}
102	
103	######################################################################
104	# Public Functions (bujb_*)
105	
106	# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint.
107	#
108	# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
109	# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
110	# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that
111	# BURP_VICEROYALTY refers to a registered BURN profile by file presence).
112	#
113	# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
114	# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted),
115	# then publishes BUJB_RESOLVED_* globals as readonly. Single-call-per-process;
116	# subsequent calls die.
117	bujb_resolve_investiture() {
118	  zbujb_sentinel
119	  zburc_sentinel
120	  zburn_sentinel
121	  zburp_sentinel
122	
123	  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
124	    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"
125	
126	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE"
127	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"
128	
129	  BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY="${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
130	  BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST="${BURN_HOST}"
131	  BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM="${BURN_PLATFORM}"
132	  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER="${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
133	  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE="${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
134	  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE="${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
135	  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
136	
137	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY
138	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST
139	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM
140	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER
141	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE
142	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
143	  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER
144	
145	  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
146	}
147	
148	# bujb_command_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
149	# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w (echoed to stdout).
150	bujb_command_for() {
151	  zbujb_sentinel
152	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
153	  case "${z_letter}" in
154	    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
155	    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
156	    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
157	    *) buc_die "bujb_command_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
158	  esac
159	}
160	
161	# bujb_workload_keypath_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload privkey
162	# remote destination path (relative to workload home) for b, c, or w.
163	bujb_workload_keypath_for() {
164	  zbujb_sentinel
165	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
166	  case "${z_letter}" in
167	    b) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_b}" ;;
168	    c) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_c}" ;;
169	    w) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_w}" ;;
170	    *) buc_die "bujb_workload_keypath_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
171	  esac
172	}
173	
174	######################################################################
175	# Internal: Garrison helpers
176	
177	zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
178	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
179	  case "${z_letter}" in
180	    b|c|w) ;;
181	    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
182	  esac
183	}
184	
185	# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM
186	# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
187	zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
188	  zbujb_sentinel
189	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
190	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
191	  case "${z_letter}" in
192	    b)
193	      case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
194	        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
195	        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'" ;;
196	      esac
197	      ;;
198	    c|w)
199	      test "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
200	        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'"
201	      ;;
202	  esac
203	}
204	
205	# zbujb_workload_home LETTER -- echo the absolute workload home path on the
206	# remote node for the given shell-letter.
207	zbujb_workload_home() {
208	  zbujb_sentinel
209	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
210	  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
211	  case "${z_letter}" in
212	    b)
213	      case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
214	        bubep_linux) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
215	        bubep_mac)   echo "/Users/${z_wlu}" ;;
216	        *)           buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: unsupported platform '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'" ;;
217	      esac
218	      ;;
219	    c|w) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
220	    *)   buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: invalid letter '${z_letter}'" ;;
221	  esac
222	}
223	
224	# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
225	# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
226	# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
227	# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
228	zbujb_admin_exec() {
229	  zbujb_sentinel
230	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
231	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
232	
233	  local z_remote_invoker
234	  case "${z_letter}" in
235	    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
236	    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
237	    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
238	  esac
239	
240	  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
241	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
242	      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
243	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
244	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
245	      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
246	      "${z_remote_invoker}"
247	}
248	
249	######################################################################
250	# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})
251	
252	# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
253	zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
254	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
255	  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"
256	
257	  local z_exit=0
258	  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
259	  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
260	    case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
261	      bubep_windows)
262	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY}"
263	        ;;
264	      *)
265	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}' first."
266	        ;;
267	    esac
268	  fi
269	}
270	
271	# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
272	zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
273	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
274	  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
275	  local z_wlhome
276	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
277	  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"
278	
279	  case "${z_letter}" in
280	    b)
281	      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
282	set -uo pipefail
283	sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
284	sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
285	SCRIPT
286	      ;;
287	    c)
288	      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
289	      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
290	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
291	set -uo pipefail
292	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
293	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
294	rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
295	SCRIPT
296	      ;;
297	    w)
298	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
299	set -uo pipefail
300	userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
301	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
302	SCRIPT
303	      ;;
304	  esac
305	}
306	
307	# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
308	zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
309	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
310	  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
311	  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"
312	
313	  case "${z_letter}" in
314	    b)
315	      case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
316	        bubep_linux)
317	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
318	set -euo pipefail
319	sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
320	sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
321	SCRIPT
322	          ;;
323	        bubep_mac)
324	          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
325	          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
326	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
327	set -euo pipefail
328	sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
329	sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
330	SCRIPT
331	          ;;
332	      esac
333	      ;;
334	    c)
335	      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
336	      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
337	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
338	set -euo pipefail
339	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
340	mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
341	mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
342	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
343	SCRIPT
344	      ;;
345	    w)
346	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
347	set -euo pipefail
348	useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
349	passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
350	SCRIPT
351	      ;;
352	  esac
353	}
354	
355	# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
356	# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
357	zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
358	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
359	  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
360	  local z_wlhome
361	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
362	  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"
363	
364	  local z_command_directive
365	  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for "${z_letter}")
366	
367	  local z_pubkey
368	  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
369	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
370	
371	  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"
372	
373	  case "${z_letter}" in
374	    b|w)
375	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
376	set -euo pipefail
377	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
378	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
379	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
380	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
381	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
382	SCRIPT
383	      ;;
384	    c)
385	      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
386	      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
387	      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
388	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
389	set -euo pipefail
390	mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
391	chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
392	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
393	chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
394	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
395	SCRIPT
396	      ;;
397	  esac
398	}
399	
400	# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
401	# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
402	zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
403	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
404	  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
405	  local z_wlhome
406	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
407	  local z_keypath
408	  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
409	  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
410	  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"
411	
412	  local z_key_b64
413	  z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
414	    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
415	
416	  case "${z_letter}" in
417	    b|w)
418	      local z_sudo=""
419	      test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo="sudo -n"
420	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
421	set -euo pipefail
422	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
423	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
424	echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
425	${z_sudo} mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
426	${z_sudo} install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
427	SCRIPT
428	      ;;
429	    c)
430	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
431	set -euo pipefail
432	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
433	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
434	echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
435	mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
436	cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
437	chmod 600  '${z_target}'
438	chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
439	SCRIPT
440	      ;;
441	  esac
442	}
443	
444	# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
445	zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
446	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
447	  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"
448	
449	  local z_exit=0
450	  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
451	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
452	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
453	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
454	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
455	      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
456	      true                                        \
457	    || z_exit=$?
458	
459	  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
460	    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
461	}
462	
463	######################################################################
464	# Public: Garrison ceremony
465	
466	# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
467	# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
468	bujb_garrison() {
469	  zbujb_sentinel
470	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
471	    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
472	
473	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
474	  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"
475	
476	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"
477	
478	  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
479	  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
480	  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
481	  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
482	  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
483	  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"
484	
485	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
486	}
487	
488	# eof
489

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
#
# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
# hardening directive set.
#
# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation. A future
# PowerShell sibling would mint as bujp_jurisdiction.ps1.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUJB_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)

zbujb_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"

  # Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
  # Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
  # behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
  # requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
  BUJB_command_b='command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
  BUJB_command_c='command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
  BUJB_command_w='command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'

  # Shell-letter -> workload privkey destination path on the remote
  # (relative to the workload account home directory).
  BUJB_workload_keypath_b='.ssh/id_ed25519'
  BUJB_workload_keypath_c='.ssh/id_ed25519'
  BUJB_workload_keypath_w='.ssh/id_ed25519'

  # Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
  BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'

  # Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
  # fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
  # bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
  BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
PasswordAuthentication no
PermitEmptyPasswords no'

  readonly BUJB_command_b BUJB_command_c BUJB_command_w
  readonly BUJB_workload_keypath_b BUJB_workload_keypath_c BUJB_workload_keypath_w
  readonly BUJB_wsl_distribution
  readonly BUJB_sshd_hardening

  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
}

zbujb_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
}

# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label.
zbujb_check_key_file() {
  local z_path="${1:-}"
  local z_varname="${2:-}"

  test -n "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
  test -f "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_mode=""
  if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
    : # macOS / BSD stat
  elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
    : # GNU/Linux stat
  else
    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path}"
  fi
  test "${z_mode}" = "600" || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"

  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path}"
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (bujb_*)

# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole load-and-cross-validate entrypoint.
#
# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that
# BURP_VICEROYALTY refers to a registered BURN profile by file presence).
#
# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted),
# then publishes BUJB_RESOLVED_* globals as readonly. Single-call-per-process;
# subsequent calls die.
bujb_resolve_investiture() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  zburc_sentinel
  zburn_sentinel
  zburp_sentinel

  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"

  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE"
  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"

  BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY="${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
  BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST="${BURN_HOST}"
  BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM="${BURN_PLATFORM}"
  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER="${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE="${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE="${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"

  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY
  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST
  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM
  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER
  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE
  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
  readonly BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER

  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
}

# bujb_command_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w (echoed to stdout).
bujb_command_for() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
    *) buc_die "bujb_command_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# bujb_workload_keypath_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload privkey
# remote destination path (relative to workload home) for b, c, or w.
bujb_workload_keypath_for() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_b}" ;;
    c) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_c}" ;;
    w) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_w}" ;;
    *) buc_die "bujb_workload_keypath_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison helpers

zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|c|w) ;;
    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM
# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'" ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c|w)
      test "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'"
      ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_workload_home LETTER -- echo the absolute workload home path on the
# remote node for the given shell-letter.
zbujb_workload_home() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
        bubep_mac)   echo "/Users/${z_wlu}" ;;
        *)           buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: unsupported platform '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'" ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c|w) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
    *)   buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: invalid letter '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
zbujb_admin_exec() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"

  local z_remote_invoker
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
  esac

  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      "${z_remote_invoker}"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})

# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"

  local z_exit=0
  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
    case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
      bubep_windows)
        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY}"
        ;;
      *)
        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}' first."
        ;;
    esac
  fi
}

# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
    w)
      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux)
          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
          ;;
        bubep_mac)
          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
SCRIPT
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    w)
      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"

  local z_command_directive
  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for "${z_letter}")

  local z_pubkey
  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"

  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  local z_keypath
  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"

  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      local z_sudo=""
      test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo="sudo -n"
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
${z_sudo} mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
${z_sudo} install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
chmod 600  '${z_target}'
chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || z_exit=$?

  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
}

######################################################################
# Public: Garrison ceremony

# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_garrison() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"

  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
}
