#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
#
# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
bujb_resolve() {
  buc_doc_brief "Resolve an investiture and display the BUJB_RESOLVED_* state"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY        = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST               = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM           = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER      = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
}

# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
bujb_knock() {
  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY})"

  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY}"

  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
}

# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
bujb_command_file() {
  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local z_command_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}"
  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      'bash -s'                                   \
      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
      > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stdout.log"           \
      2> "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"          \
    || z_exit=$?

  echo "${z_exit}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/exitcode"

  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"

  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
  buc_step "Capture complete"
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY})"

  exec ssh -t                                     \
      -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      "bash -i"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zbujb_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burp_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle
  zbujb_kindle

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_burp_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burp.env"
  test -f "${z_burp_file}" || buc_die "BURP profile not found: ${z_burp_file}"
  source "${z_burp_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURP: ${z_burp_file}"
  zburp_kindle
  zburp_enforce

  # Cross-reference: BURP_VICEROYALTY must resolve to a registered BURN profile.
  local -r z_burn_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/${BURP_VICEROYALTY}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_burn_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile referenced by BURP_VICEROYALTY=${BURP_VICEROYALTY} not found: ${z_burn_file}"
  source "${z_burn_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_burn_file}"
  zburn_kindle
  zburn_enforce
}

buc_execute bujb_ "Bash Utility Jurisdiction" zbujb_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"

  # Privileged Regime subsystem (burp_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_VALIDATE "buw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "burp_validate" "param1" "Validate BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_RENDER   "buw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "burp_render"   "param1" "Render BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_LIST     "buw-rpl" "${z_mod}" "burp_list"     ""       "List BURP profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)

content.text:
Here's what I've gathered for L3d (fenestrate ceremony) and how it fits the existing module:

**Current shape (post-L3c, commit fb1429dc):**
- `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` houses `BUJB_sshd_hardening` (locked spec data), `zbujb_admin_exec` (central remote dispatcher with `BatchMode=yes` for key-only auth), and the 6-step garrison ceremony.
- `bujb_cli.sh` has `bujb_garrison_{bash,cygwin,wsl}`, `bujb_knock`, `bujb_command_file`, `bujb_interactive_session`, `bujb_resolve` (diagnostic).
- All garrison tabtargets (`buw-jpG[bcw]`) and workload tabtargets (`buw-jw{k,c,s}`) are wired.
- `buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh` does **not** exist yet — net-new for L3d.

**L3d Plan — the structural piece that diverges from garrison:**

Fenestrate's phase-1 admin SSH is fundamentally different from garrison's — it uses `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` with **interactive password fallback on /dev/tty**. `zbujb_admin_exec` is hardcoded to `BatchMode=yes` (key-only). I'll add a sibling `zbujb_admin_exec_interactive` (no BatchMode, with publickey,password preferred auth) and use it for phase-1 only. Phase-2 uses the existing `zbujb_admin_exec` (key-only).

**Phase-1 disconnect at `Restart-Service sshd`:** the SSH session dies mid-call. The bash orchestrator must treat *any* exit code from the phase-1 final exec as the expected boundary (the script will not return cleanly). I'll structure phase-1 as a single chained heredoc that does `place pubkey → icacls → write sshd_config → verify → sshd -t → Restart-Service sshd`, where the last line being executed is the restart, and we wrap the whole exec in `|| true` since the disconnect produces nonzero exit. Then phase 2 reconnects under key-only and runs verification (post-state check + a no-op true).

**Concrete shape:**
1. `zbujb_admin_exec_interactive LETTER` — same as `zbujb_admin_exec` but `-o BatchMode=no -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`, allows /dev/tty password prompt.
2. `zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform` — asserts `BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM == bubep_windows` (single-letter; no per-shell variation).
3. `zbujb_fenestrate_phase1` — single heredoc: derive admin pubkey via `ssh-keygen -y` locally, embed via base64 + decode on remote, append to `administrators_authorized_keys` if not present (idempotent), `icacls` lockdown, write hardening directives idempotently, PowerShell `Get-Content` + bash-side parse verify, `sshd -t`, then `Restart-Service sshd`. Wrapped to swallow disconnect.
4. `zbujb_fenestrate_phase2` — reconnect via `zbujb_admin_exec` (key-only); run a verify script that re-reads `sshd_config` and asserts the three hardened directives are still effective.
5. `bujb_fenestrate()` — public orchestrator: assert ZBUJB_RESOLVED, assert platform, run phase 1, sleep briefly (let sshd come back), run phase 2.
6. `bujb_fenestrate` command in `bujb_cli.sh` (BUZ_FOLIO check + resolve + fenestrate).
7. Zipper enrollment `BUWZ_JP_FENESTRATE buw-jpF bujb_cli.sh bujb_fenestrate param1 "Fenestrate — admin SSH key trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"`.
8. `tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh` (logged tabtarget — capture trace useful for debugging the two-phase ceremony).

**Notes:**
- "Bash-side BCG-compliant matching" per BUSJPF step 5: I'll use `grep -E '^[[:space:]]*(PubkeyAuthentication|PasswordAuthentication|PermitEmptyPasswords)[[:space:]]+'` against the raw bytes returned from PowerShell's `Get-Content`, then for each directive assert the effective value matches the hardening intent.
- I'll thread an `apt`-style "convergent" posture: the phase-1 idempotent ops (pubkey append, sshd_config write) check first, only mutate on need.
- `BUJB_PRIVILEGED_USER` may contain spaces (paddock standing note line 207); I'll quote at every expansion.

**No silks rename needed** — pace silks `implement-jurisdiction` still fits; L3d is a layer of that work.

Sequential opus — single file delicate work (bash, SSH session lifetimes, PowerShell quoting). No parallelization.

# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_garrison() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"

  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Fenestrate helpers (BUSJPF — Windows OpenSSH only)
#
# Phase 1 uses an SSH ControlMaster session with publickey,password preferred
# auth so the operator types the admin password at most once (first run).
# Steady-state runs authenticate by key without any prompt. The master is
# reused across the two phase-1 atomic ops; a single trap closes it on exit.
# Phase 2 is a fresh key-only SSH session that survives independent of the
# pre-restart auth state.

ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL=""

zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}'"
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_close_master -- idempotent master cleanup. Safe to call
# from EXIT trap; never errors, never calls buc_die.
zbujb_fenestrate_close_master() {
  if test -n "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}" && test -S "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}"; then
    ssh -S "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}" -O exit \
        "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  if test -n "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}"; then
    rm -rf "$(dirname "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}")" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL=""
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_open_master -- open an SSH ControlMaster session with
# publickey,password preferred auth. Operator types admin password on
# /dev/tty if key auth fails (first-run path). Sets EXIT trap.
zbujb_fenestrate_open_master() {
  local z_ctl_dir
  z_ctl_dir=$(mktemp -d -t bujb_fenestrate.XXXXXX) \
    || buc_die "Failed to create master socket dir"
  ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL="${z_ctl_dir}/master.sock"

  trap 'zbujb_fenestrate_close_master' EXIT

  ssh -M -S "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}"                     \
      -o ControlMaster=yes                                 \
      -o ControlPersist=60                                 \
      -o BatchMode=no                                      \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
      -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
      -N -f                                                \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
    || buc_die "Phase 1 master SSH failed (auth — verify BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER and admin password are correct on ${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_via_master -- read PowerShell script from stdin and
# run it on the remote node over the open master. Default Windows OpenSSH
# shell is cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke powershell -NoProfile -File - to
# read the script body from stdin.
zbujb_fenestrate_exec_via_master() {
  test -n "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}" || buc_die "Fenestrate master not open"
  ssh -S "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_CTL}" \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly -- read PowerShell script from stdin and
# run it on the remote node over a fresh key-only SSH session (phase 2).
zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly() {
  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
      -o BatchMode=yes                                     \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey                \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives BYTES -- assert each hardened directive
# in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value in BYTES (raw
# sshd_config contents from PowerShell Get-Content). CRLF-tolerant.
zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives() {
  local z_bytes="${1:-}"
  local z_clean
  z_clean=$(printf '%s' "${z_bytes}" | tr -d '\r')

  local z_pair z_directive z_expected z_actual
  while IFS= read -r z_pair; do
    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
    z_directive="${z_pair%% *}"
    z_expected="${z_pair#* }"

    z_actual=$(printf '%s\n' "${z_clean}" \
      | grep -E "^[[:space:]]*${z_directive}[[:space:]]+" \
      | head -n 1 \
      | awk '{print $2}')

    test -n "${z_actual}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Fenestrate phases

# zbujb_fenestrate_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
# expected — exit code ignored).
zbujb_fenestrate_phase1() {
  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Open master SSH (publickey,password fallback — operator may be prompted)"
  zbujb_fenestrate_open_master

  local z_pubkey
  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Install admin pubkey + icacls + merge sshd_config + sshd -t + emit"

  local z_remote_bytes z_exit=0
  z_remote_bytes=$(zbujb_fenestrate_exec_via_master <<PS1
\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
\$sshConfig    = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config"

# Idempotent admin pubkey install
if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
}
\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
}

# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant 'SYSTEM:F' /grant 'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Idempotent sshd_config harden — for each directive replace the first
# matching line (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
\$directives = [ordered]@{
  'PubkeyAuthentication'   = 'yes'
  'PasswordAuthentication' = 'no'
  'PermitEmptyPasswords'   = 'no'
}

\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
\$seen  = @{}
foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
  \$matched = \$false
  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
      }
      \$matched = \$true
      break
    }
  }
  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
}
foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
}
Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII

# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op of phase 1; aborts before restart on bad config
& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (must be the last op so output is clean)
Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
PS1
) || z_exit=$?
  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}) — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${z_remote_bytes}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — last atomic op)"
  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_via_master <<'PS1' || true
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
Restart-Service sshd
PS1

  zbujb_fenestrate_close_master
  trap - EXIT
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
zbujb_fenestrate_phase2() {
  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"

  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
  sleep 3

  local z_remote_bytes z_exit=0
  z_remote_bytes=$(zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly <<'PS1') || z_exit=$?
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config" -Raw
PS1
  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}) — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart, or sshd did not come back up"

  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${z_remote_bytes}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Fenestrate ceremony

# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_fenestrate() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform

  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"

  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2

  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
}

# eof

}

# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant 'SYSTEM:F' /grant 'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Idempotent sshd_config harden — for each directive replace the first
# matching line (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
\$directives = [ordered]@{
  'PubkeyAuthentication'   = 'yes'
  'PasswordAuthentication' = 'no'
  'PermitEmptyPasswords'   = 'no'
}

\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
\$seen  = @{}
foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
  \$matched = \$false
  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
      }
      \$matched = \$true
      break
    }
  }
  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
}
foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
}
Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII

# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op of phase 1; aborts before restart on bad config
& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (must be the last op so output is clean)
Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
PS1
) || z_exit=$?
  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}) — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${z_remote_bytes}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — last atomic op)"
  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_via_master <<'PS1' || true
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
Restart-Service sshd
PS1

  zbujb_fenestrate_close_master
  trap - EXIT
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
zbujb_fenestrate_phase2() {
  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"

  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
  sleep 3

  local z_remote_bytes z_exit=0
  z_remote_bytes=$(zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly <<'PS1') || z_exit=$?
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config" -Raw
PS1
  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}) — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart, or sshd did not come back up"

  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${z_remote_bytes}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Fenestrate ceremony

# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_fenestrate() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform

  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST})"

  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2

  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
}

# eof

+# Phase 2 is a fresh key-only SSH session that survives independent of the

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
#
# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
bujb_resolve() {
  buc_doc_brief "Resolve an investiture and display the BUJB_RESOLVED_* state"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY        = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST               = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM           = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PLATFORM}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BUJB_RESOLVED_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER      = ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}"
}

# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
bujb_knock() {
  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY})"

  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY}"

  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
}

# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
bujb_command_file() {
  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local z_command_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}"
  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      'bash -s'                                   \
      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
      > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stdout.log"           \
      2> "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"          \
    || z_exit=$?

  echo "${z_exit}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/exitcode"

  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"

  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
  buc_step "Capture complete"
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST} (${BUJB_RESOLVED_VICEROYALTY})"

  exec ssh -t                                     \
      -i "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      "${BUJB_RESOLVED_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BUJB_RESOLVED_HOST}" \
      "bash -i"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zbujb_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burp_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle
  zbujb_kindle

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_burp_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burp.env"
  test -f "${z_burp_file}" || buc_die "BURP profile not found: ${z_burp_file}"
  source "${z_burp_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURP: ${z_burp_file}"
  zburp_kindle
  zburp_enforce

  # Cross-reference: BURP_VICEROYALTY must resolve to a registered BURN profile.
  local -r z_burn_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/${BURP_VICEROYALTY}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_burn_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile referenced by BURP_VICEROYALTY=${BURP_VICEROYALTY} not found: ${z_burn_file}"
  source "${z_burn_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_burn_file}"
  zburn_kindle
  zburn_enforce
}

buc_execute bujb_ "Bash Utility Jurisdiction" zbujb_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"

  # Privileged Regime subsystem (burp_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_VALIDATE "buw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "burp_validate" "param1" "Validate BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_RENDER   "buw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "burp_render"   "param1" "Render BURP profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RP_LIST     "buw-rpl" "${z_mod}" "burp_list"     ""       "List BURP profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Handbook — top index + Windows OS procedures (buhw_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhw_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_H0_TOP            "buw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_handbook_top"        ""        "BUK top-level handbook index"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_TOP            "buw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_top"                ""        "BUK-level Windows procedures checklist"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE    "buw-HWab"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_base"        ""        "OpenSSH server install + lockdown"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE  "buw-HWar"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_remote"      ""        "Client key generation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY   "buw-HWax"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_entrypoints" ""        "SSH command= routing + icacls"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL        "buw-HWew"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_wsl"    "param1"  "WSL distro creation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN     "buw-HWec"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_cygwin" ""        "Cygwin install"

  # Handbook — jurisdiction (buhj_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhj_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HJ0_TOP           "buw-hj0"   "${z_mod}" "buhj_top"                ""        "Jurisdiction handbook landing + admin SSH bootstrap"

  # Jurisdiction operational — workload ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bujb_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_KNOCK          "buw-jwk"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_knock"               "param1"  "Knock — probe workload SSH reachability"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_COMMAND_FILE   "buw-jwc"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_command_file"        "param1"  "Run command file as workload, capture outputs"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JW_INTERACTIVE    "buw-jws"   "${z_mod}" "bujb_interactive_session" "param1"  "Interactive SSH session as workload"

  # Jurisdiction operational — garrison ceremonies (bujb_cli.sh)
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_BASH    "buw-jpGb"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_bash"     "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter b: native bash, Linux/Mac)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_CYGWIN  "buw-jpGc"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_cygwin"   "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter c: Cygwin bash, Windows)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_JP_GARRISON_WSL     "buw-jpGw"  "${z_mod}" "bujb_garrison_wsl"      "param1"  "Garrison workload (shell-letter w: WSL bash, Windows)"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

# eof

content.content:
1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	See LICENSE for terms.
5	-->
6	
7	# Bash Console Guide (BCG) - Pattern Reference
8	
9	## Core Philosophy
10	
11	This pattern emerged from environments where hidden failures cascade into production outages. When bash scripts manage critical infrastructure, every potential failure point needs explicit handling at its location, making post-mortem analysis straightforward.
12	
13	The aesthetic of elegant bash pipelines trades debuggability for brevity. A failing command in `$(echo | sed | awk)` leaves no trace. Temp files make each transformation step visible, testable, and recoverable.
14	
15	Traps and signal handlers create action-at-a-distance effects that obscure control flow. When error handling lives at the point of failure rather than in distant handlers, code review and maintenance become predictable.
16	
17	Enterprise bash isn't about looking clever; it's about being boringly, reliably correct. These patterns optimize for the 3am debugging session, not the initial writing experience. Each external process spawn is a potential failure point, so bash builtins are preferred despite verbosity.
18	
19	The patterns reject bash 4+ features to maintain compatibility with older enterprise systems still running bash 3.2, particularly older RHEL/CentOS or macOS deployments.
20	
21	### Load-Bearing Complexity
22	
23	Every element in a system — every function extraction, every pattern variant, every structural distinction — must carry weight. An element is **load-bearing** when its removal would create a gap between intent and behavior. When similar things differ, ask whether the difference is load-bearing: if yes, document why; if no, homogenize.
24	
25	This is the general decision criterion that tools like Zeroes Theory instantiate. Zeroes Theory asks "how many zeroes did this add to state space?" — load-bearing complexity asks the prior question: "does this element earn its existence at all?"
26	
27	Concrete examples from this codebase:
28	
29	- **Four separate `rbgi_add_*_iam` functions** look like copy-paste, but each wraps a different GCP API with different failure modes. The variation is load-bearing — extracting a "generic IAM helper" would hide which API failed at 3am.
30	- **Three copy-pasted Secret Manager IAM blocks** are NOT load-bearing variation — same API, same failure class, same recovery. Extract a helper.
31	- **Not adding three-part structure to a simple `api_enable` spec definition** — the structure would add ceremony without carrying meaning. Non-load-bearing elements increase cognitive cost without increasing correctness.
32	
33	Non-load-bearing elements are the silent budget overruns of a codebase. Each one is small; their aggregate is what makes systems incomprehensible.
34	
35	## Module Architecture
36	
37	Every module has an implementation file. CLI entry points are only present if module requires standalone execution.
38	
39	```bash
40	«prefix»_«name».sh (implementation - REQUIRED, prefer single lowercase word, allow snake_case)
41	«prefix»_cli.sh (entry point - OPTIONAL, omit for library/utility modules)
42	```
43	
44	### CLI as Module Gateway
45	
46	**Rule**: Code outside the BCG module system must never call `z*_kindle()` directly. All external access to BCG modules goes through CLI scripts.
47	
48	**Pattern**:
49	```
50	External Code → CLI script → furnish() kindles → module functions
51	```
52	
53	**Why this matters**:
54	- Kindle sequences are implementation details that may change
55	- The CLI's `furnish()` owns the kindle graph (which modules to kindle, in what order)
56	- External code remains stable when module dependencies change
57	- CLIs provide the documented, stable API for module functionality
58	
59	**Anti-pattern**:
60	```bash
61	# ❌ External script kindling directly
62	source "${TOOLS_DIR}/rbob_bottle.sh"
63	zrbob_kindle                          # VIOLATION: kindle leaked outside CLI
64	rbob_charge
65	```
66	
67	**Correct pattern**:
68	```bash
69	# ✅ External script delegates to CLI
70	exec "${TOOLS_DIR}/rbob_cli.sh" rbob_charge "$@"
71	```
72	
73	**What counts as "external code"**:
74	- Entry point scripts (workbenches, testbenches, coordinators)
75	- TabTargets and launchers
76	- Any dispatcher or router that invokes module functionality
77	- Scripts that are not themselves BCG modules
78	
79	**What can call kindle**:
80	- A module's own CLI (in `furnish()`)
81	- Another CLI's `furnish()` (when CLI A depends on module B, A's furnish kindles B)
82	- Test harnesses that explicitly isolate module behavior
83	
84	**Filesystem marker**: The `_cli.sh` suffix and the executable bit must agree. Within a BCG module directory:
85	
86	- Every `*_cli.sh` MUST be executable
87	- Every other `*.sh` MUST NOT be executable
88	
89	This makes the gateway role visible to `ls -l`: a workbench, testbench, or implementation module carrying `+x` is a bug — direct execution bypasses the CLI's `furnish()` and the kindle graph it owns. Tabtargets in `tt/` and shipped tool binaries are not BCG modules and stand outside this rule.
90	
91	### Module-CLI Prefix Unity
92	
93	**Convention**: A module and its CLI share a prefix (`rbrn_regime.sh` + `rbrn_cli.sh`).
94	
95	When commands within a single CLI have different dependency weights (e.g., `validate` needs
96	only the regime file, `audit` needs GCP/OAuth), use **differential furnish**: the furnish
97	function receives the command name as `$1` and conditionally sources/kindles heavy
98	dependencies based on which command is being invoked.
99	
100	### Regime Module Archetype
101	
102	A **regime** is a sourced `.env` file containing typed configuration fields — the contract between human-authored configuration and the modules that consume it. When a module's purpose is validating a regime, it follows the regime archetype:
103	
104	| Component | Location | Purpose |
105	|-----------|----------|---------|
106	| `buv_*_enroll` calls | kindle | Declare field contracts (type, range, description) |
107	| `buv_scope_sentinel` | kindle (last, before KINDLED) | Catch undeclared `PREFIX_*` variables |
108	| `z«prefix»_enforce` | regime module | `buv_vet SCOPE` + custom format checks |
109	| `readonly` lock | after enforce | Lock enrolled variables against mutation |
110	| `*_validate` command | CLI | `buv_report` — explained per-field display |
111	| `*_render` command | CLI | `buv_render` — diagnostic dump |
112	
113	Furnish sequence: kindle → enforce → lock (ironclad gate before any command runs). After enforce succeeds, lock all enrolled `PREFIX_*` variables with `readonly` to prevent accidental mutation by downstream code. Any derived state (e.g., docker env arrays) must be built from validated values after enforce, before or during the lock step.
114	
115	**Singleton** regimes source config unconditionally in furnish. **Manifold** regimes (multiple instances, e.g. nameplates) source conditionally when a folio identifies the instance.
116	
117	Enrollment types and gating API: see `buv_validation.sh`.
118	
119	---
120	
121	## Function Patterns
122	
123	### Boilerplate Functions (one per module/CLI)
124	
125	| Function             | Location       | First Line                                         | Can Source?       | Can use buc_step? | Purpose                                          |
126	|----------------------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
127	| `z«prefix»_kindle`   | Implementation | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" \|\| buc_die`    | Yes (credentials) | No (use buc_log_*)| Define all kindle constants, set module state    |
128	| `z«prefix»_sentinel` | Implementation | `test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" \|\| buc_die` | No                | No                | Guard all other functions                        |
129	| `z«prefix»_enforce`  | Implementation | `z«prefix»_sentinel`                               | No                | No                | Validate sourced config (regime archetype only)  |
130	| `z«prefix»_furnish`  | CLI only       | `buc_doc_env...`                                   | Yes (all deps)    | No                | Document env vars, source all deps, kindle       |
131	| Module header        | Implementation | `test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" \|\| buc_die`    | No                | N/A               | Prevent multiple inclusion                       |
132	| CLI header           | CLI            | `set -euo pipefail`                                | Yes (buc_command)  | N/A               | Source buc_command.sh only                       |
133	
134	**Kindle constant**: Any variable — internal (`Z«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) or public (`«PREFIX»_SCREAMING_NAME`) — defined exclusively within the kindle function with `readonly`. No other function — including enroll, setup, or helper functions — may assign to kindle constants. This ensures module state is fully determined at kindle time and visible in one place.
135	
136	**Mutable kindle state**: A variable initialized in kindle but intentionally mutated after kindle returns (counters, accumulators, registry rolls, builder state). Uses **lowercase** `z_«prefix»_name` to visually distinguish from `readonly` kindle constants. Never apply `readonly` to mutable kindle state.
137	
138	**Tinder constant**: A public variable (`«PREFIX»_lower_name`) defined at module top level, immediately after the `Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1` guard. Tinder constants must resolve to pure string literals at source time — **no runtime dependency, no `$(…)` computation, no expansion of kindle constants or runtime variables**. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is permitted: a tinder value may interpolate `${«PREFIX»_lower_name}` for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves the no-runtime-dependency invariant. They are available immediately after sourcing — no kindle required. Use `SCREAMING` case for the prefix (module identity) and `lower_snake` case for the name to visually distinguish from kindle constants. This enables sourcing chains where a tinder constant from module A provides the path for sourcing module B's config in the furnish function. The name reflects their role in the kindle metaphor: tinder is the fixed material prepared before kindling — inert, unchanging, and enabling the fire to start.
139	
140	**KINDLED must be last**: `readonly Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1` must be the final statement in kindle. Since sentinel checks this variable, setting it last guarantees all kindle constants, roll arrays, and enroll calls are complete before the module is considered operational. Any function calling sentinel before kindle finishes will correctly fail. The `readonly` makes re-kindling a loud error.
141	
142	### Regular Functions (output via printouts/temp files)
143	
144	This table defines scope: «prefix»_* is public, z«prefix»_* is internal.
145	
146	| Type            | Pattern              | First Line           | Parameters | buc_step | Documentation        |
147	|-----------------|----------------------|----------------------|------------|----------|----------------------|
148	| Public          | `«prefix»_«command»` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `"${1:-}"` | Yes      | Required (buc_doc_*) |
149	| Internal helper | `z«prefix»_«name»`   | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `"${1:-}"` | Yes      | Recommended (buc_doc_*) |
150	
151	---
152	
153	## File Templates
154	
155	### Template 1: Shebang and Copyright
156	
157	```bash
158	#!/bin/bash
159	#
160	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
161	#
162	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
163	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
164	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
165	#
166	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
167	#
168	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
169	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
170	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
171	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
172	# limitations under the License.
173	#
174	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
175	#
176	```
177	
178	### Dispatch-Provided Directory Variables
179	
180	Three BURD variables carry directory paths across the exec boundary into CLI processes. These are derived from BURC configuration and exported by bud_dispatch.sh during zbud_setup():
181	
182	| Variable | Source | Value |
183	|----------|--------|-------|
184	| `BURD_TOOLS_DIR` | `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` | Project tools root directory |
185	| `BURD_BUK_DIR` | Derived from `BURC_TOOLS_DIR` | BUK subdirectory (`$BURC_TOOLS_DIR/buk`) |
186	| `BURD_TABTARGET_DIR` | `BURC_TABTARGET_DIR` | Tabtarget directory |
187	
188	These variables are available in exec'd CLI processes without requiring the CLI to re-source BURC. CLIs that need stable paths to tools or utilities should use these variables rather than reconstructing paths from `BASH_SOURCE`.
189	
190	### Template 2: CLI Entry Point
191	
192	BUD variables are provided by dispatch. The CLI header sources only `buc_command.sh`; all other dependencies are sourced inside the furnish function. This enables doc-mode help display without runtime env vars — furnish gates after `buc_doc_env` calls before any sourcing that might fail.
193	
194	```bash
195	# «shebang»
196	# «copyright»
197	
198	set -euo pipefail
199	
200	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
201	
202	z«prefix»_furnish() {
203	
204	  # Document only the BUD variables actually used
205	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
206	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
207	  buc_doc_env "«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE " "Module specific configuration file"
208	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
209	
210	  # Source all dependencies
211	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
212	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/«prefix»_«name».sh"
213	
214	  # Source regime files, if any
215	  source "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source regime file"
216	
217	  z«prefix»_kindle
218	  z«prefix»_enforce  # Regime archetype: ironclad gate after kindle (omit if no regime)
219	}
220	
221	buc_execute «prefix»_ "«module_description_phrase»" z«prefix»_furnish "$@"
222	
223	# eof
224	```
225	
226	### Differential Furnish (conditional dependency sourcing)
227	
228	The furnish function receives the **command name** as `$1`, enabling conditional sourcing based on which command will execute. This allows heavy dependencies (e.g., GCP client libraries, OAuth flows) to be loaded only for commands that need them.
229	
230	**Pattern**: Light dependencies always loaded; heavy dependencies conditionally sourced per command.
231	
232	```bash
233	z«prefix»_furnish() {
234	  local z_command="${1:-}"
235	
236	  # Document environment variables
237	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
238	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
239	  buc_doc_env "«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE " "Module configuration file"
240	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
241	
242	  # Always source light dependencies
243	  source "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source regime"
244	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source validation"
245	
246	  # Conditionally source heavy dependencies based on command
247	  case "${z_command}" in
248	    «prefix»_survey|«prefix»_audit)
249	      # These commands need GCP and OAuth infrastructure
250	      source "${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/some/heavy_dep.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source heavy deps"
251	      zheavy_kindle
252	      ;;
253	    «prefix»_validate|«prefix»_render)
254	      # These commands work with config only
255	      ;;
256	  esac
257	
258	  z«prefix»_kindle
259	}
260	```
261	
262	**Key points:**
263	- The furnish function receives the command name (e.g., "«prefix»_validate") as `$1`
264	- Existing furnish functions that don't use `$1` are unaffected (extra arg ignored in bash)
265	- Light, always-needed dependencies are sourced unconditionally
266	- Heavy dependencies are sourced only for the commands that need them (avoiding startup overhead)
267	- The `case` statement can be omitted if a module has no conditional dependencies
268	
269	**Benefits:**
270	- Faster startup for lightweight commands (validate, render, list)
271	- Heavy infrastructure (GCP SDKs, OAuth flows) loaded only when actually needed
272	- Reduced memory footprint for simple operations
273	- Clear visibility of which commands depend on what infrastructure
274	
275	### Template 3: Implementation Module
276	
277	```bash
278	# «shebang»
279	# «copyright»
280	
281	set -euo pipefail
282	
283	# Multiple inclusion detection
284	test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
285	Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1
286	
287	# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition — available at source time)
288	# «PREFIX»_some_path="fixed-filename.env"
289	# «PREFIX»_some_prefix="prefix_"
290	
291	######################################################################
292	# Internal Functions (z«prefix»_*)
293	
294	z«prefix»_kindle() {
295	  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» already kindled"
296	
297	  # Validate only the BURD variables actually used
298	  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"   # If using temp files
299	  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"  # If producing outputs
300	  test -n "${BURD_NOW_STAMP:-}" || buc_die "BURD_NOW_STAMP is unset"  # If using timestamps
301	
302	  # Validate module specific environment
303	  buv_file_exists "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}"  # If present
304	
305	  # Source credentials into environment only - never write to disk
306	  source "${REGIME_CRED_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source credentials"
307	
308	  # Kindle constant (public — safe to read from other modules; never a secret)
309	  «PREFIX»_PUBLIC_SELECTED_NAME="some-specific-name"
310	
311	  # Kindle constants (internal)
312	  Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_temp.txt"  # Non-secret data only
313	  Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_result.json"
314	  Z«PREFIX»_CONFIG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_config_"     # rest of file name appended on use
315	  Z«PREFIX»_MANIFEST_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_manifest_"   # rest of file name appended on use
316	
317	  # Temporary files (if using BURD_OUTPUT_DIR)
318	  Z«PREFIX»_OUTPUT_RESULT="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/result.txt"
319	
320	  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
321	}
322	
323	z«prefix»_sentinel() {
324	  test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module «prefix» not kindled - call z«prefix»_kindle first"
325	}
326	
327	# Helper functions follow
328	
329	######################################################################
330	# External Functions («prefix»_*)
331	
332	«prefix»_«command»() {
333	  z«prefix»_sentinel
334	
335	  local z_param1="${1:-}"
336	  local z_param2="${2:-}"
337	
338	  # Documentation block
339	  buc_doc_brief "«one_line_description»"
340	  buc_doc_param "«required_arg»" "«one_line_description»"
341	  buc_doc_oparm "«optional_arg»" "«one_line_description»"
342	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
343	
344	  # Implementation
345	  test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "Parameter 'param1' is required"
346	
347	  buc_step "«user_visible_milestone»"
348	  buc_log_args "«detailed_progress_information»"
349	  [command] || buc_die "«failure_explanation»"
350	
351	  buc_success "«success_message»"
352	}
353	```
354	
355	**Kindle constant — anti-pattern:**
356	
357	```bash
358	# ❌ Kindle constant defined outside kindle
359	z«prefix»_setup() {
360	  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"   # VIOLATION: must be in kindle
361	}
362	
363	# ✅ Kindle constants defined in kindle, used elsewhere
364	z«prefix»_kindle() {
365	  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"
366	  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
367	}
368	```
369	
370	**Tinder vs kindle constant — choosing correctly:**
371	
372	```bash
373	# ✅ Tinder constant: pure string, available at source time
374	«PREFIX»_config_file="config.env"        # lower_snake name — no kindle needed
375	
376	# ✅ Tinder-on-tinder composition: expansion of earlier tinder constant
377	«PREFIX»_role_director="director"
378	«PREFIX»_fact_ext_director="ext-${«PREFIX»_role_director}"  # resolves at source time
379	
380	# ✅ Kindle constant: depends on runtime state, requires kindle
381	«PREFIX»_CONFIG_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # SCREAMING name — kindle required
382	
383	# ❌ Wrong: runtime variable expansion in tinder position
384	«PREFIX»_config_dir="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # VIOLATION: BURD_TEMP_DIR is runtime — must be kindle
385	
386	# ❌ Wrong: computation in tinder position
387	«PREFIX»_color_red=$(z«prefix»_color '1;31')   # VIOLATION: $() — must be in kindle
388	
389	# ❌ Wrong: expansion of kindle constant in tinder position
390	«PREFIX»_derived="${«PREFIX»_KINDLED_VAR}/sub"  # VIOLATION: KINDLED_VAR available only after kindle
391	```
392	
393	## Variable Handling (General Rules)
394	
395	### Expansion Requirements
396	
397	**Always use braced, quoted expansion:**
398	```bash
399	"${z_var}"              # Required pattern - prevents word splitting and glob expansion
400	"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
401	"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
402	```
403	
404	**Never use:**
405	```bash
406	$var                    # Unquoted - word splitting risk
407	"$var"                  # Unbraced - boundary ambiguity
408	${var}                  # Unquoted - glob expansion risk
409	```
410	
411	**No tilde in quoted strings** — tilde expansion is suppressed inside quotes:
412	```bash
413	# ❌ Tilde does not expand in quotes
414	"~/Downloads/file.txt"          # Literal ~ character, not home directory
415	
416	# ✅ Use $HOME instead
417	"${HOME}/Downloads/file.txt"    # Expands correctly
418	```
419	
420	**Quote inside parameter expansion operators:**
421	```bash
422	# ✅ Inner expansion quoted separately — matches literally
423	"${z_path%"${z_suffix}"}"       # Strip suffix
424	"${z_path#"${z_prefix}"}"       # Strip prefix
425	
426	# ❌ Inner expansion unquoted — matches as a glob pattern
427	"${z_path%${z_suffix}}"         # z_suffix with * or ? chars matches as glob!
428	"${z_path#${z_prefix}}"         # Same risk
429	```
430	
431	When using `%`, `%%`, `#`, `##` operators with a variable as the pattern, the inner expansion must be quoted separately. Without inner quotes, the pattern is glob-expanded — a suffix containing `*` or `?` silently matches more than intended.
432	
433	### Assignment Patterns
434	
435	```bash
436	# Direct assignment
437	local z_message="Processing item"
438	
439	# ❌ NEVER declare multiple variables on one line
440	local z_name z_value z_result  # Hidden initialization failures!
441	
442	# ✅ ALWAYS one declaration per line with explicit initialization
443	local z_name=""
444	local z_value="default"
445	local z_result=0
446	
447	# ❌ NEVER use local -i — silently coerces non-integer values to 0
448	local -i z_count="${z_input}"  # "abc" becomes 0 — violates no-silent-failures!
449	
450	# ✅ Use plain local with explicit validation
451	local z_count="${z_input}"
452	test "${z_count}" -ge 0 2>/dev/null || buc_die "z_count must be non-negative integer, got: ${z_count}"
453	
454	# ✅ Two-line pattern for captured values (explicit and debuggable)
455	local z_token
456	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
457	
458	# ❌ NEVER combine declaration with capture
459	local z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture)  # Hides exit status!
460	
461	# From file using builtin $(<file) - NOT command substitution, a bash builtin
462	local z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}")
463	test -n "${z_content}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE}"
464	
465	# Never split declaration from $(<file) assignment
466	# ❌ local z_content
467	# ❌ z_content=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_FILE}")  # Unnecessary verbosity
468	
469	# Use Capture functions for secrets that must never touch disk
470	local z_token
471	z_token=$(zauth_get_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to capture token"
472	
473	# Temp files INSTEAD of command substitution holds intermediate results
474	echo "${z_result}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE}"
475	complex_command > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex command failed"
476	process < "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP1}" > "${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
477	local z_result=$(<"${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}")
478	test -n "${z_result}" || buc_die "Failed to read or empty: ${Z«PREFIX»_TEMP2}"
479	```
480	
481	### Prefer Bash Builtins Over External Tools
482	
483	```bash
484	# ✅ String manipulation with parameter expansion
485	"${z_path##*/}"                    # Instead of: basename
486	"${z_path%/*}"                     # Instead of: dirname
487	"${z_string//old/new}"             # Instead of: sed 's/old/new/g'
488	"${z_string#prefix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/^prefix//'
489	"${z_string%suffix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/suffix$//'
490	
491	# ✅ Reading lines with 'read' — guard handles missing trailing newline
492	while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
493	  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo line"
494	done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"
495	
496	# ✅ Arithmetic with $((...))
497	z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))    # Instead of: expr or bc
498	```
499	
500	### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution

file.content:
<!--
